steps.title: Use the table to find the quadratic
steps.content: Write the quadratic as $f(x)=ax^2+bx+c$. From $f(0)=16$, we get $c=16$. The other two rows give $$a+b+16=24$$ and $$a-b+16=10.$$ So $a+b=8$ and $a-b=-6$, which gives $a=1$ and $b=7$.

steps.title: Match the model to the choices
steps.content: The equation $f(x)=x^2+7x+16$ fits the table. A quick substitution confirms it: $f(-1)=1-7+16=10$, $f(0)=16$, and $f(1)=1+7+16=24$. All three table rows match, so D is correct.
